how it works: Fits a linear function by minimizing residual sum of squares between predictions and observed targets.
tasks best at: Baseline regression, interpretable coefficient estimates, roughly linear relationships.

how it works: Linear regression with L2 regularization that shrinks coefficients to reduce variance and multicollinearity problems.
tasks best at: Linear problems with correlated predictors, many moderately useful features, stable prediction.

how it works: Linear regression with L1 regularization that can shrink some coefficients exactly to zero.
tasks best at: Feature selection, sparse models, interpretability when only a subset of predictors matters.

how it works: Combines L1 and L2 penalties so it both shrinks coefficients and can zero some out.
tasks best at: High-dimensional data with correlated predictors where you want both stability and feature selection.

how it works: Learns one sparse coefficient pattern jointly across multiple related target variables.
tasks best at: Multi-output regression when the same predictors should matter across several outcomes.

how it works: Joint multi-output linear model with combined L1 and L2 penalties.
tasks best at: Multi-output problems with correlated predictors and shared structure across outcomes.

how it works: Builds a linear model by adding predictors gradually along the direction most correlated with the residual.
tasks best at: High-dimensional linear problems, especially when you want a full path of sparse solutions efficiently.

how it works: Uses the LARS procedure to fit Lasso-type sparse linear models.
tasks best at: Sparse high-dimensional settings where LARS is computationally attractive.

how it works: Greedy sparse regression that repeatedly picks the feature that most reduces residual error and refits on the selected set.
tasks best at: Very sparse signal recovery, compressed sensing style problems, small subsets of truly relevant features.

how it works: Bayesian linear regression with priors over coefficients, yielding shrinkage and uncertainty-aware estimates.
tasks best at: Linear problems where regularization is needed and probabilistic coefficient estimates are useful.

how it works: Bayesian linear model with separate relevance priors per feature, aggressively shrinking irrelevant ones.
tasks best at: Sparse linear regression with automatic feature relevance determination.

how it works: Generalized linear model with a log link and Poisson-distributed target assumption.
tasks best at: Count outcomes such as events, visits, or occurrences that are nonnegative integers.

how it works: Generalized linear model for positive continuous targets with Gamma-distributed noise and a log link.
tasks best at: Positive, right-skewed continuous outcomes like costs, durations, or insurance claims.

how it works: Generalized linear model covering several target distributions through a power parameter.
tasks best at: Positive data, zero-inflated positive data, or other outcomes that fit the Tweedie family.

how it works: Fits a linear model using stochastic gradient descent on one sample or minibatch at a time.
tasks best at: Very large or streaming datasets, sparse data, online learning.

how it works: Linear model optimized with Huber loss, which is quadratic for small errors and linear for large ones.
tasks best at: Data with moderate outliers where you still want a mostly linear model.

how it works: Repeatedly fits a base model on random subsets and keeps the fit with the largest consensus set of inliers.
tasks best at: Datasets with many gross outliers or contaminated observations.

how it works: Robust linear estimator based on medians over many subsample slopes.
tasks best at: Small-to-medium datasets needing robust linear fits under outliers.

how it works: Fits a chosen conditional quantile rather than the conditional mean using pinball loss.
tasks best at: Asymmetric prediction, heteroskedastic data, prediction intervals, lower- or upper-tail modeling.

how it works: Expands features into polynomial terms, then fits a linear model in the expanded feature space.
tasks best at: Nonlinear but still smooth relationships when you want a simple interpretable extension of linear regression.

how it works: Applies ridge regression in a kernel-defined feature space, allowing nonlinear fits without explicit feature expansion.
tasks best at: Nonlinear regression on small-to-medium datasets where a smooth kernelized fit works well.

how it works: Fits a margin-based regression function that ignores small errors inside an epsilon tube and penalizes larger ones.
tasks best at: Nonlinear regression on smaller datasets, especially when kernel methods help.

how it works: Variant of SVR controlled by nu, which sets bounds related to support vectors and margin errors.
tasks best at: Similar use cases as SVR when nu is a more convenient tuning parameter.

how it works: Linear-margin support vector regression optimized for larger datasets than kernel SVR.
tasks best at: Large-scale linear regression problems, especially with many features.

how it works: Predicts from the average or distance-weighted average target of the k nearest training points.
tasks best at: Local nonlinear structure, low-dimensional data, flexible decision boundaries with enough data.

how it works: Predicts using training points that fall within a fixed radius of the query point.
tasks best at: Problems with uneven sample density where a fixed neighborhood radius is more natural than fixed k.

how it works: Places a Gaussian process prior over functions and uses a kernel to model smoothness and uncertainty.
tasks best at: Small datasets, smooth nonlinear functions, uncertainty quantification, Bayesian optimization.

how it works: Projects predictors and targets into shared latent components that maximize their covariance, then regresses in that space.
tasks best at: Many correlated predictors, multicollinearity, small-n large-p settings.

how it works: Recursively splits the feature space into regions and predicts a constant value within each terminal leaf.
tasks best at: Nonlinear interactions, mixed feature types, interpretable rule-based regression.

how it works: Fits many decision trees on bootstrapped samples with feature randomness and averages their predictions.
tasks best at: Strong default nonlinear regression, tabular data, interactions, low tuning burden.

how it works: Builds many highly randomized trees and averages them to reduce variance.
tasks best at: Fast, strong tabular regression when extra randomness helps generalization.

how it works: Sequentially fits weak learners, reweighting attention toward examples previous models predicted poorly.
tasks best at: Improving simple base regressors, moderate-size tabular problems.

how it works: Builds an additive ensemble stage by stage by fitting each tree to the negative gradient of the loss.
tasks best at: High-performance tabular regression, nonlinear effects, custom losses, moderate dataset sizes.

how it works: A faster gradient boosting variant that bins features into histograms before fitting trees.
tasks best at: Medium-to-large tabular datasets, strong predictive performance, monotonic constraints.

how it works: Fits many copies of a base regressor on random subsets and averages them.
tasks best at: Variance reduction for unstable base learners like decision trees.

how it works: Combines several different regressors by averaging their predictions.
tasks best at: Simple ensembling when multiple diverse models each perform reasonably well.

how it works: Uses predictions from base regressors as inputs to a final regressor that learns how to combine them.
tasks best at: Blending complementary models for better accuracy on tabular data.

how it works: Fits a non-decreasing or non-increasing piecewise-constant function under an order constraint.
tasks best at: One-dimensional monotonic relationships and calibration-style problems.

how it works: Feed-forward neural network trained by backpropagation to learn flexible nonlinear mappings.
tasks best at: Complex nonlinear patterns in tabular data when you have enough data and careful tuning.
